summary: Large IF-module decompilation PR centered on Sound Test, with additional work in if_2F72 and ifmagnify. The PR replaced many placeholder declarations and `/// #` stubs with C implementations, typed prototypes, shared struct fields, and support header declarations. decomp.dev reported +5388 matched bytes, +0.14% overall matched code, 57 new matches, and 31 partial-match improvements. Review accepted the progress while noting that some code remained rough, especially unrolled loops.

key_files.role: Main body of the PR; added many Sound Test implementations, static data placeholders, extern data references, and calls into audio, card, snapshot, and SObj systems.
Converted many `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations into typed prototypes for Sound Test functions.

Added implementations for animation/audio/GObj status helper functions around `fn_802F7288`, `if_802F73C4`, `if_802F74D0`, `fn_802F75D4`, `fn_802F7670`, `fn_802F770C`, `fn_802F77F8`, `fn_802F7994`, and player-slot entry helpers.
Typed prototypes for newly implemented if_2F72 functions and added `if/forward.h` to reference `Element_803F9628`.
Implemented several magnify-camera/object lifecycle functions, including camera creation, cleanup, background color setup, archive load/init, and ignore-offscreen toggles.
Replaced unknown magnify prototypes with concrete signatures and exposed `ifMagnify_804D57E8`.
Refined `Element_803F9628` layout with an x12 bitfield struct and callback typedef.
Made `ifStatus_803F9628` non-static and updated its initializers to match the refined struct layout.
Included `if/types.h` and exported `ifStatus_803F9628[8]` for if_2F72 use.
Extended `HSD_SObj_803A477C_t` with `x40`, needed by Sound Test snapshot/SObj code.

what_changed: Implemented a large batch of Sound Test functions in `src/melee/if/soundtest.c`, replacing many `/// #function` stubs with C bodies and adding many local static data buffers and extern data declarations.
Added many typed Sound Test prototypes in `soundtest.h`, replacing broad `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations with `int`, `s32`, `bool`, `void`, pointer, and argument-specific signatures.

Implemented IF status/GObj animation helpers in `if_2F72.c`, including functions that animate JOBJs, trigger audio at frame thresholds via `lbAudioAx_800237A8`, call per-entry callbacks, create/destroy GObjs, and manage slot-indexed state in `lbl_804A1340`.

Recovered orphaned original ROM strings in `if_2F72.c`, including `ScInfStc_scene_models` and `translate`; `if_802F7E24` now passes the recovered string symbol instead of the literal archive-section string.

Implemented several magnify functions in `ifmagnify.c`: camera object setup with ortho/viewport/scissor, cleanup of player magnify GObjs, ground-color/default setup, archive load/init across six slots, and toggles for `ignore_offscreen`.
Refined `Element_803F9628` in `types.h`: split prior broad fields into `x10`, `x11`, `x12` bitfields, `x13`, and callback-typed `x18`/`x1C` fields.
Changed `ifStatus_803F9628` from static internal storage to an exported symbol declared in `ifstatus.h`, allowing new if_2F72 code to scan and update the status-entry array.
Added an `x40` byte to `HSD_SObj_803A477C_t`, used by new Sound Test code that sets `sobj->x40 |= 2` after creating an SObj.
smart_moves: Typed `Element_803F9628` just enough to support matching code paths: small frame bytes, a named x12 bitfield bundle, and callback function-pointer fields rather than leaving all entries as raw integers.
Exporting `ifStatus_803F9628` through `ifstatus.h` avoided duplicating or guessing state in `if_2F72.c` and made the newly implemented helpers operate on the real status-entry table.
Preserved original-data evidence by naming orphaned ROM strings and reusing `lbl_803F9780` in the archive load call, which can help data-section matching.
Used targeted matching controls in Sound Test, including comments that functions would otherwise inline `un_802FFD94` and `#pragma dont_inline` around groups of tiny wrapper functions.

Kept rough but match-effective source patterns, including pointer arithmetic and explicit slot searches/unrolled assignments, where the reviewer acknowledged the code quality tradeoff was acceptable for match progress.
Added minimal baselib struct surface area (`HSD_SObj_803A477C_t.x40`) only where required by observed code, rather than over-modeling the whole SObj type.
decomp_lessons: For progress-heavy IF work, converting headers from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to concrete prototypes can unlock implementation and cross-file type checking even when names remain address-based.
When a function needs access to another module's data table, prefer exporting the real symbol with the recovered struct type over creating duplicate local declarations.
Bitfields can be useful for matching packed flag bytes like `Element_803F9628.x12`, especially when individual bits gate one-shot audio/callback behavior.
Original orphaned strings are useful matching anchors; keep them as data symbols and route calls through them when the binary did.
Small wrapper families may require `#pragma dont_inline` to prevent the compiler from inlining shared helpers and disrupting matches.
Unrolled loops and verbose repeated assignments may be accepted in decomp PRs when they materially improve matching, but reviewers may flag them as rough and future cleanup candidates.
naming_conventions: Unidentified IF functions retained address-based names such as `fn_802F7288`, `if_802F73C4`, `un_803004E8`, and `fn_80300CC8`.
Global/static unknown data kept address-derived names such as `lbl_804A1340`, `un_803FA258`, `un_804D6DC8`, and `ifMagnify_804A1DE0`.
Recovered original strings were represented as `lbl_803F9780`/`lbl_803F9798` with a comment noting they are orphaned data strings from the original ROM.
Struct fields remained offset-style (`x0`, `x10`, `x12`, `x18`, `x40`) when semantics were not confidently known.
Callback typedef was named module-specifically as `IfStatusCb`, reflecting its use in `Element_803F9628` entries.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma dont_inline` around Sound Test wrapper functions that call `un_802FFD94`, with an explicit comment that they would otherwise inline the helper.
Used a local `ST_MIN` macro inside `un_802FFEE0` to express a min operation and then immediately undefined it.
Used pointer arithmetic over typed arrays and byte casts to reproduce slot-index and struct-offset calculations, for example computing an `Element_803F9628` index from pointer subtraction.
Used explicit six-slot repeated assignments for magnify `ignore_offscreen` toggles instead of loop abstraction.
Used a volatile local default value in `ifMagnify_802FC7C0`, likely to preserve compiler behavior around loading `ifMagnify_804DDB60`.
Preserved exact GObj/JObj lifecycle ordering: create GObj, load JObj, attach object, setup GX link, request/animate, set translation, mark matrix dirty, and install process callbacks.

review_feedback.comment: Some of this is pretty rough, for example there are a lot of unrolled loops. But I can't argue with the match progress.
Reported matched code 48.32% (+0.14%, +5388 bytes), 57 new matches, and 31 improvements in unmatched functions.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that clean up unrolled loops in `soundtest.c`, `if_2F72.c`, or `ifmagnify.c` after PR #2075.
Search for later renames or semantic typing of `Element_803F9628`, `Element_803F9628_x12`, and `IfStatusCb`.
Check whether `HSD_SObj_803A477C_t.x40` receives a semantic field name in later baselib work.
Search for later Sound Test PRs that replace `un_803FA258`, `un_803FA128`, or `un_804D6DC8` address-style globals with named structs.
